title: Vysoká Lhota

infobox.value: is a municipality and village in [Pelhřimov District](/wiki/Pelh%C5%99imov_District "Pelhřimov District") in the [Vysočina Region](/wiki/Vyso%C4%8Dina_Region "Vysočina Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). As of 2025, it has 16 inhabitants, making it the least populated municipality of the country.

["Historický lexikon obcí České republiky 1869–2011"](https://csu.gov.cz/produkty/historicky-lexikon-obci-1869-az-2015) (in Czech). [Czech Statistical Office](/wiki/Czech_Statistical_Office "Czech Statistical Office"). 2015-12-21.

**Vysoká Lhota** is a municipality and village in [Pelhřimov District](/wiki/Pelh%C5%99imov_District "Pelhřimov District") in the [Vysočina Region](/wiki/Vyso%C4%8Dina_Region "Vysočina Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). As of 2025, it has 16 inhabitants, making it the least populated municipality of the country.

Vysoká Lhota lies approximately 13 kilometres (8 mi) west of [Pelhřimov](/wiki/Pelh%C5%99imov "Pelhřimov"), 40 km (25 mi) west of [Jihlava](/wiki/Jihlava "Jihlava"), and 87 km (54 mi) south-east of [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague").

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vysok%C3%A1_Lhota&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Demographics")]

Vysoká Lhota is the least populated municipality of the Czech Republic. With an average age of 64.3, it is also the municipality with the oldest population.[[1]](#cite_note-pop-1)

Historical population

| Year | Pop. | ±% |

| 1869 | 178 | — |
| 1880 | 176 | −1.1% |

| 1910 | 186 | +23.2% |
| 1921 | 177 | −4.8% |
| 1930 | 186 | +5.1% |
| 1950 | 103 | −44.6% |
| 1961 | 102 | −1.0% |
| 1970 | 84 | −17.6% |
| 1980 | 56 | −33.3% |
| 1991 | 43 | −23.2% |
| 2001 | 26 | −39.5% |
| 2011 | 17 | −34.6% |
| 2021 | 16 | −5.9% |
| Source: Censuses[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) | | |
